mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% across 50+ observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power with t-stat below 1.5 when unemployment is below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate revenue forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production above the 2017 baseline index of 100.0 reflects robust manufacturing and capacity utilization that supports near-term earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO exceeds 100.0 in >70% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and wage growth that directly increase aggregate consumer demand, creating a persistent positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS levels and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 across all tested rolling windows, or returns turn negative during high-payroll months.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 320.0 signal entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity risk premiums and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 320.0, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical significance in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic momentum that lifts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses predictive power when GDP growth decouples from consumption, or forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered cases over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A tight labor market characterized by unemployment below 4.0% combined with rising payrolls sustains wage-driven consumption growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive short-term returns when unemployment drops below 4.0% but payrolls stagnate, or the joint effect shows negative alpha during supply-shock episodes.
